body.title: Every non-library class inherits the base — the base IS the logging and lifecycle layer
body.statement: Device classes inherit BaseDevice (unseen_university/device.py); shim classes inherit BaseShim (unseen_university/shim.py); both chains carry DiagnosticBase + CoreValuesMixin, which is where self.log, the canonical per-device log hierarchy, lifecycle verbs (start/stop/restart/self_test/rollback), and the CP1-CP6 contract come from. A non-library class outside the hierarchy, or module-level functions doing device work, is a failure. Exempt: pure data classes, exceptions, test fixtures, and library code with no device role.
body.why: Uniform lifecycle and logging come from inheritance — the rack can start, stop, and self-test every device without knowing its internals, and every bare except has a self.log handle to report through. A class outside the hierarchy is invisible to the rack and silent when it fails.
